step_log.result: Registration reached a confirmation-code page and required email verification
step_log.evidence: First-party registration remained pending until email verification

step_log.action: Open a new browser session and register or log in with Google OAuth using the same email
step_log.result: OAuth registration completed without requiring email verification
step_log.evidence: Google OAuth session reached an activated account state

step_log.action: Log in with the email/password credentials from the first registration
step_log.result: Email/password login reached the same account created or linked through OAuth
step_log.evidence: The post-login account identifier matched the OAuth session identifier

step_log.action: Compare account identifiers from OAuth and email/password sessions
step_log.evidence: The same redacted API key was observed from both sessions

llm_result.reasoning: Google OAuth completed without email verification and later email/password login reached the same account.

rule_result.reason: First-party registration required verification, OAuth completed, email login completed, and both paths resolved to identical identifiers.

path_a.reason: First-party registration required verification, OAuth completed, email login completed, and both paths resolved to identical identifiers.

path_b.reasoning: Google OAuth completed without email verification and later email/password login reached the same account.

screenshot_evidence.interpretation: Screenshot verification unavailable (no screenshots or OCR dependencies not installed)

attack_path: Registered via email/password and reached an email verification requirement
Started a new browser session and used Google OAuth with the same email
OAuth registration completed without email verification
Email/password login later reached the same account
evidence.missing_security_check: OAuth registration should not auto-link to a pending unverified first-party registration without verifying ownership.
evidence.proof: The same redacted API key was observed from the OAuth session and the email/password session.
